MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-16-2024 Set For Trial On Jul-07-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,400; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Erik Harper, Esq.
Off Calendar Dec-09-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Erik Harper, Esq. 310-550-1220 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

ARGUED. The Court notes Defendant sent a contest email yesterday but did not appear to contest today. Mr. Harper submits to the tentative ruling. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LASHEA RODRIGUEZ's Motion To Compel Defendant'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,400.

Plaintiff Lashea Rodriguez's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories 12.3 and 12.6 is granted.

Plaintiff filed a separate statement that complies with CRC 3.1345 and her meet and confer declaration is sufficient.

The subject interrogatories seek the identification of documents, some of which may contain privileged information. Defendant University of California San Francisco correctly notes that it was not obligated to produce a privilege log. (Hernandez v. Superior Court (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 285, 293.) "But the existence of a document containing privileged information is not privileged. [citations] Interrogatories may be used to discover the existence of documents in the other party's possession. [citations] If an interrogatory asks the responding party to identify a document, an adequate response must include a description of the document." (Id.) Here, Defendant's amended responses generally refer to documents covered by the attorney-client and work product privileges. Defendant does not adequately describe these documents, however. Defendant shall provide supplemental responses that adequately describe the allegedly privileged documents by January 9, 2025.

The record does not support a finding that Defendant acted with substantial justification. The court orders sanctions in favor of Plaintiff and against Defense Counsel in the amount of $1,100.

Mr. Harper to submit a proposed order.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
